Name: latency_optimized_deep_learning_inference
Title: Latency-Optimized Deep Learning Inference on Commodity GPUs via Adaptive Mixed-Precision Quantization and Efficient Memory Access
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that by combining adaptive mixed-precision quantization with efficient memory access patterns and optimized kernel implementations, we can significantly reduce the latency of deep learning inference on commodity GPUs while maintaining accuracy.
Related Work: Recent works such as SNIP, HiLoRA, EMQ, and AMP-ViT have explored mixed-precision quantization and training-free domain generalization for efficient deep learning inference. However, these works focus on specific aspects of the problem and do not provide a comprehensive solution that addresses both quantization and memory access. Our proposal builds upon these works and aims to provide a holistic approach to latency-optimized deep learning inference on commodity GPUs.
Abstract: Deep learning models are increasingly being deployed on edge devices and commodity GPUs, where latency and memory constraints are significant challenges. To address these challenges, we propose a latency-optimized deep learning inference framework that combines adaptive mixed-precision quantization with efficient memory access patterns and optimized kernel implementations. Our framework, which we call LatencyOpt, uses a novel quantization-aware memory allocation scheme to minimize memory access overhead and maximizes the use of GPU resources. We evaluate LatencyOpt on a range of deep learning models and datasets, including ResNet, MobileNet, and ImageNet, and demonstrate significant reductions in latency and memory usage while maintaining accuracy.
Experiments: Evaluate the performance of LatencyOpt on a range of deep learning models and datasets
Compare the latency and memory usage of LatencyOpt with state-of-the-art mixed-precision quantization methods
Investigate the impact of different quantization schemes and memory access patterns on latency and accuracy
Optimize the kernel implementations and memory allocation scheme to further improve performance
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of the proposed framework may lead to increased development and deployment costs
The use of adaptive mixed-precision quantization may require significant tuning and calibration to achieve optimal results
The framework may not be compatible with all deep learning models and datasets, and may require significant modifications to accommodate specific use cases
